name: Skater Bounds
instructions: Start in an athletic stance on one leg, knee slightly bent.
Push laterally off the ground leg and bound sideways onto the opposite leg.
Absorb the landing softly with a bent knee, hold briefly for stability, then immediately bound back the other way.
Keep the trunk stable and drive with the hips, mimicking a skating stride.

name: Copenhagen Plank
instructions: Lie on your side with your top leg's shin or foot resting on a bench, bottom leg free.
Support your upper body on your forearm, hips lifted in a side-plank position.
Hold, keeping hips square and level, for the prescribed time.
Regress by bending the bottom knee and resting it on the floor if the full hold is too hard.

name: Lateral Band Walk
instructions: Place a mini-band around your ankles or just above the knees.
Sit into a quarter-squat position and keep tension on the band throughout.
Step sideways with control, avoiding letting the trailing foot drag or the knees cave in.
Complete the prescribed distance/reps in one direction, then repeat the other way.

name: Monster Walk
instructions: Place a band around your ankles and get into an athletic quarter-squat stance.
Step forward and slightly out on a diagonal, alternating legs, keeping constant band tension.
Keep the chest up and avoid rotating the hips as you step.

name: Single-Leg RDL
instructions: Stand on one leg holding a dumbbell in the opposite hand.
Hinge at the hips, extending the free leg straight back as your torso lowers toward parallel with the floor.
Keep the standing knee soft and the back flat throughout.
Drive the hips forward to return to standing, maintaining balance.

name: Depth Jump
instructions: Step off a low box (30-45cm) and land on both feet, absorbing the impact with bent knees.
Immediately explode upward into a maximal vertical jump on landing — minimize ground contact time.
Reset and repeat. Prioritize quality of the reactive jump over reps.

name: Broad Jump
instructions: Start in a quarter-squat with arms back.
Swing the arms forward and explosively jump forward as far as possible, driving through the hips.
Land softly on both feet with bent knees, sticking the landing before resetting.

name: Banded Hip Thrust
instructions: Sit with upper back against a bench, band around the hips, feet flat on the floor.
Drive through the heels and extend the hips upward against the band tension until torso is in line with thighs.
Squeeze the glutes hard at the top, then lower with control.

name: Split Squat Hold (Isometric)
instructions: Step into a split stance with the back heel lifted.
Lower into a lunge position until the back knee hovers just above the floor.
Hold this position, keeping the torso upright and weight balanced between both legs, for the prescribed time.
Switch legs and repeat.

name: Shift-Simulation Intervals
instructions: Choose a conditioning modality (bike, rower, or shuttle sprints).
Work at a hard, sustained effort for 40-45 seconds to mimic a hockey shift.
Rest 2-4 minutes to mimic bench time, then repeat for the prescribed number of shifts.
Keep effort high and consistent across all rounds rather than pacing evenly.

name: Rotational Medicine Ball Throw
instructions: Stand side-on to a solid wall, feet shoulder-width apart, holding a medicine ball at hip height.
Rotate away from the wall to load, then explosively rotate through the hips and throw the ball into the wall.
Catch the rebound (or reset it) and repeat for reps, then switch sides.

name: Band Woodchop
instructions: Anchor a band high and stand side-on to the anchor point.
Pull the band down and across your body in a chopping motion, rotating through the trunk and hips.
Control the return to the start position and repeat, then switch sides.

name: Hip Mobility Flow (90/90, Pigeon)
instructions: Sit in the 90/90 position — front leg bent 90°, back leg bent 90° behind you — and lean gently forward over the front shin.
Rotate through to switch sides, pivoting on your hips.
Move into pigeon pose, extending the back leg straight behind and sinking the hips toward the floor.
Cycle slowly between positions, holding each for a few breaths.

name: Single-Leg Balance Work
instructions: Stand on one leg with a soft knee, arms relaxed at your sides.
Hold the position, resisting any wobble, for the prescribed time.
Progress by closing your eyes or adding small reaching movements with the free leg.
Switch sides and repeat.

name: Thoracic Rotation Stretch
instructions: Start on all fours, one hand behind your head.
Rotate the elbow down and across toward the opposite arm, then open back up and rotate the chest toward the ceiling.
Move slowly and with control through the full range, following the elbow with your eyes.
Complete the prescribed reps, then switch sides.

name: Sprint Mechanics Drill (A-Skips)
instructions: Skip forward, driving one knee up to hip height while the opposite arm drives forward.
Land on the ball of the foot under the hips, then quickly cycle to the next rep.
Focus on a tall posture and crisp knee drive over covering distance quickly.

name: Single-Leg Hop-and-Stick
instructions: Balance on one leg, then hop forward a short distance.
Land on the same leg and stick the landing for 2-3 seconds without wobbling before the next rep.
Keep the landing knee tracking over the toes, not caving inward.
Complete the reps on one leg, then switch.

name: Shoulder External Rotation (Band)
instructions: Anchor a light band at elbow height and hold it with the elbow tucked at your side, bent 90°.
Rotate the forearm outward away from your body, keeping the elbow pinned to your side.
Control the return to the start position.
Complete reps, then switch arms.

name: Rotational Core Work (Pallof Press)
instructions: Stand side-on to a band anchor, holding the band at your chest with both hands.
Press the band straight out in front of you, resisting the pull rotating you toward the anchor.
Hold briefly, then return to your chest with control.
Complete reps, then switch sides.

name: Lateral Shuffle / Carioca
instructions: Shuffle laterally in an athletic stance for the prescribed distance, staying low and avoiding crossing the feet.
On the return, perform carioca (grapevine) steps — rotating the hips and crossing one foot over then behind the other.
Keep the movement quick and controlled, maintaining an athletic posture throughout.

name: Wrist & Forearm Work
instructions: Rest your forearm on a bench or your thigh, wrist hanging free, holding a light dumbbell.
Curl the wrist up and lower with control for wrist flexion, then flip the grip for extension.
Keep movements slow and controlled — this is a rehab/durability exercise, not a max-effort lift.
Complete reps for both flexion and extension, then switch arms.
